mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns do not decline when rates rise.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in real GDP growth below 1.0% annualized signals demand contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 1.0% annualized
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances when GDPC1 growth drops below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in unemployment and declines in total employment indicate labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending power, causing negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces no statistically significant negative alpha (p > 0.05) compared to single-series baselines over walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side weakness that accelerates recession fears, driving negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO declines during tightening periods (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Increases in retail sales reflect strong consumer demand that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month changes show zero predictive power for 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, borrowing costs severely constrain corporate leverage and consumer credit, producing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of observations when FEDFUNDS crosses the 4.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment during periods of elevated inflation signals stagflationary pressure that forces aggressive central bank tightening, causing negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when inflation is high, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising housing starts and falling retail sales indicates shifting demand toward durable goods that temporarily boosts construction-related equities, generating positive short-term market returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between HOUST and RSAFS yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample regression tests.
